assertions.title: Index Daily Data Covers 5 Years for All 8 Indices

steps.action: Run SELECT symbol_code, MIN(trade_time), MAX(trade_time), COUNT(*) FROM klines WHERE symbol_type='INDEX' AND timeframe='DAY' AND symbol_code IN (...) GROUP BY symbol_code
steps.expected: 8 rows, each with min <= 2021-01-08 and count >= 1100 (except 899050.BJ)
steps.observed: 8 rows returned. 7 indices have min=2021-01-04, count=1264. 899050.BJ has min=2022-12-19, count=789 (expected — BSE 50 launched late 2022)

assertions.title: Index Daily Data Has No Significant Gaps

steps.action: Cross-reference trade_calendar trading days with klines for 7 indices (2021-01-04 to 2026-03-25)
steps.observed: 0 missing trading days for all 7 indices (query returned no rows)

steps.action: Cross-reference trade_calendar with klines for 899050.BJ from 2022-12-19

assertions.title: Backfill Uses TuShare pro.index_daily() for Indices

steps.expected: Uses fetch_index_daily() which calls pro.index_daily(), not Sina
steps.observed: backfill_index() calls self.tushare_client.fetch_index_daily(). No Sina references found.

steps.action: Verify fetch_index_daily() in tushare_client.py delegates to pro.index_daily()

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed. All 8 indices have complete daily data (7 from 2021-01-04 with 1264 rows, 899050.BJ from 2022-12-19 with 789 rows). Zero gaps found for any index. Backfill script correctly uses TuShare pro.index_daily().
